metadata.note: Multilateral / Archetype B -- primary_target null. There is no subject centroid to target (AFRICA-ETHIOPIA and AFRICA-ERITREA do not exist as centroid_anchor rows), so alias purity is the only lever. Gate is fixed nouns: the Tigray/Eritrea war-risk actors plus the Egypt axis (GERD, Asmara). AFRICA-HORN is a MULTI-COUNTRY centroid (ET/ER/SO/DJ), so per vocab-spec Pillar 2 edge case the country name `Eritrea` IS legitimate sub-centroid geography here -- the centroid cannot disambiguate which Horn state a title is about. `Ethiopia` is deliberately NOT included: it would admit bus crashes, bond restructurings, Dangote fertiliser and hyenas.
pre_audit_drops: Horn of Africa - 17 titles, all 17 in scope, but they are Turkey's deepwater drilling, Djibouti-and-the-Iran-war, and 'Türkiye won't let Israel's aggression into Horn of Africa'. A THEATER-level term, not this atomic's; would cross-contaminate somalia_state_security.
Ethiopia (en) - would make this a country FN, not a friction node. Excluded on purpose.
Fano - 4 matches, 1 real (an AFP fact-check saying the article was fabricated); the rest are Stefano/Fanone/Troufanov/orfano substring garbage.
Assab - 0 titles, despite being the port Abiy is said to want.
Erythrée - 0 titles: wrong orthography, corrected to Érythrée (vocab-spec rule 6c).
Тиграй / Эритрея / 提格雷 / 厄立特里亚 / ティグレ / エリトリア - 0 titles each; RU corpus is 5 titles and CJK 0 for this theater. Kept in the bundle anyway as correct-orthography future-proofing, at zero cost.
collision_notes: TPLF and GERD are 4-char all-caps -> the acronym path (case-SENSITIVE whole word), so no 'gerd'/'tplf' substring risk.

Éthiopie / Äthiopien ride the non-EN SUBSTRING path with no boundary protection. Accepted: total FR+DE Horn coverage is 41 titles and all sampled matches are on-topic. Revisit if German/French Ethiopia coverage broadens.
